message:
🐛 Fix static asset 404s on read-only container filesystems (#2314)

When the watchdog proxy forwards requests, Go's http.Transport adds
Accept-Encoding: gzip by default. This causes Fiber's c.SendFile()
with compress=true to invoke fasthttp's built-in file compression,
which tries to create cached compressed files (.fiber.gz) on disk.
On containers with readOnlyRootFilesystem: true (our Helm default),
these writes fail and fasthttp returns 404 for files without
pre-compressed (.br/.gz) versions — including manifest.json,
favicon.ico, and several PNG/SVG assets. This prevents the app
from loading entirely.

Two fixes:
1. Remove compress=true from c.SendFile() calls in preCompressedStatic
   — the middleware already handles serving pre-compressed variants,
   so fasthttp's built-in compression is redundant
2. Set DisableCompression: true on the watchdog Transport to prevent
   the proxy from injecting Accept-Encoding: gzip into proxied requests

Fixes: vllm-d and pok-prod consoles not loading (app-shell spinner)

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -64,11 +64,16 @@ func runWatchdog(cfg WatchdogConfig) error {
 	// Create reverse proxy
 	proxy := httputil.NewSingleHostReverseProxy(backendURL)
 
-	// Custom transport with managed connection pool and timeouts
+	// Custom transport with managed connection pool and timeouts.
+	// DisableCompression prevents the Transport from adding Accept-Encoding: gzip
+	// to proxied requests. Without this, fasthttp's SendFile tries to create
+	// compressed file caches (.fiber.gz) which fails on read-only filesystems,
+	// causing 404s for static assets like manifest.json and favicon.ico.
 	proxy.Transport = &http.Transport{
 		DialContext: (&net.Dialer{
 			Timeout: watchdogHealthTimeout,
 		}).DialContext,
+		DisableCompression:    true,
 		ResponseHeaderTimeout: watchdogProxyHeaderTimeout,
 		MaxIdleConns:          watchdogMaxIdleConns,
 		MaxIdleConnsPerHost:   watchdogMaxIdleConnsPerHost,
